incidents.victim: Ukraine — State Hydrographic Service (maritime critical infrastructure)
incidents.vector: A weaponised phishing email exploiting a stored cross-site scripting flaw in Zimbra webmail (CVE-2025-66376) was sent via a compromised student account; the entire malicious payload was embedded in the email body with no attachments, executing silently when the email was opened in an active Zimbra session.
incidents.impact: Attackers silently harvested login credentials, session tokens, two-factor authentication codes, stored passwords, and up to 90 days of mailbox data from targeted officials; CISA added the vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue on 18 March and ordered federal agencies to patch within two weeks.

incidents.victim: Multiple healthcare and government organisations (Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center customers globally)
incidents.vector: Ransomware operators exploited CVE-2026-20131, a maximum-severity zero-day vulnerability in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center software, beginning 26 January — more than five weeks before Cisco's public disclosure on 4 March; Amazon's honeypot network captured the exploit traffic and CISA issued a three-day federal patch mandate.
incidents.impact: Confirmed victims include healthcare facilities (dialysis provider DaVita and Kettering Health, where chemotherapy and pre-surgery appointments were disrupted and cancer patients' data was leaked); the city of Saint Paul, Minnesota was forced to declare a state of emergency after 43 GB of files were stolen; the education sector represents the largest share of overall Interlock activity.

incidents.victim: Marquis Software (bank communication software vendor serving 74+ US financial institutions)
incidents.vector: Attackers gained unauthorised access to Marquis Software's systems in August 2025 and copied files; the breach was not publicly disclosed until November 2025 and full victim counts were only confirmed via regulatory filings on 18 March 2026.
incidents.impact: 672,075 individuals confirmed affected across at least 74 banks, credit unions, and financial institutions; stolen data includes names, addresses, Social Security numbers, Taxpayer Identification Numbers, dates of birth, and financial account information; no ransomware group claimed responsibility.

incidents.victim: University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) — Mississippi's only children's hospital and Level I trauma centre; Passaic County, New Jersey government
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — ransomware (Russia-based operation, per expert assessment)
incidents.vector: Ransomware deployed against both organisations, forcing UMMC to go fully offline for nine days, with clinical staff reverting to paper-based processes; the same group separately knocked out phone lines and IT systems across Passaic County government offices.
incidents.impact: UMMC's cancer infusion centre had to reschedule patients; the hospital fully restored systems on 2 March after a nine-day outage; $800,000 ransom demanded from each victim with a threatened data leak deadline of 20 March; county government experienced widespread communications failure.

incidents.victim: Telus Digital (BPO and digital-services subsidiary of Canadian telecom Telus, serves clients globally)
incidents.vector: Attackers used Google Cloud Platform credentials found inside data stolen in a prior third-party supply-chain breach (Salesloft/Drift), then used credential-scanning tooling to pivot laterally across Telus systems — including a large BigQuery database — over several months without detection.
incidents.impact: Up to 1 petabyte of data claimed stolen, reportedly including FBI background checks, source code, financial records, Salesforce data, and voice recordings spanning at least 28 client organisations; attackers demanded a $65 million ransom which Telus rejected; the breach remained undetected for months, exposing downstream clients of the BPO provider.

incidents.victim: Stryker Corporation (Fortune 500 medical device manufacturer, ~56,000 employees, operations in 60+ countries)
incidents.attacker: Iran — state-directed hacktivist proxies (Ministry of Intelligence-linked group)
incidents.vector: Attackers are assessed by researchers to have gained access to Stryker's Microsoft Intune mobile device management console — likely via phishing or infostealer-harvested credentials — then remotely triggered a mass factory-reset wipe across enrolled corporate devices worldwide.
incidents.impact: More than 200,000 servers, laptops, phones, and other Windows devices were wiped across offices in 79 countries; Stryker's ordering and shipping systems remained offline one week after the attack; emergency cardiac telemetry system Lifenet was temporarily reported non-functional in Maryland; Stryker stock fell more than 3%; CISA and the FBI opened investigations and described this as the first successful destructive cyberattack on a major US corporation since the start of the US-Israel-Iran war.

incidents.victim: TELUS Digital (secondary listing) / FBI-background-check ecosystem — and separately: Wisconsin ambulance provider (largest in state)
incidents.vector: Cyberattack on the largest ambulance services provider in Wisconsin compromised patient and operational data; the attack vector has not been publicly disclosed.
incidents.impact: 235,000 individuals confirmed affected; patient personal and health data exposed; CERT notifications issued to state regulators.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — data extortion / stolen-data marketplace
incidents.vector: LeakBase operated as one of the world's largest online forums for buying and selling stolen credentials and breach data, facilitating downstream attacks against organisations globally.
incidents.impact: US Department of Justice announced seizure of the LeakBase database on 4 March 2026, disrupting a major stolen-data trading hub used by cybercriminals to monetise breaches and enable credential-stuffing and account-takeover attacks.

incidents.victim: Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform (criminal infrastructure takedown affecting ~100,000 victim organisations globally)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — credential theft / adversary-in-the-middle phishing
incidents.vector: The platform operated as a transparent proxy sold via Telegram since 2023 that harvested Microsoft 365 and Gmail session cookies, allowing full multi-factor-authentication bypass and account takeover at scale; over 3 million malicious messages were attributed to the platform in February 2026 alone.
incidents.impact: A coordinated public-private takedown by Microsoft, Proofpoint, Europol, Cloudflare, and law enforcement across multiple countries resulted in seizure of 330 control-panel domains; the platform had given criminals access to nearly 100,000 organisations including schools, hospitals, and government institutions before disruption.
